mnemonics: HDFS: Hadoop's Distributed File System - think 'Hard Drive For Super-Sized' data
keyPoints: Hadoop uses a MapReduce programming model to process large datasets in parallel across distributed clusters
The Hadoop ecosystem consists of HDFS for storage and MapReduce for processing
Data is processed where it is stored to minimize data transfer across the network (data locality)
commonMistakes: Confusing Hadoop with a real-time processing system (it's designed for batch processing)
Assuming HDFS is a replacement for traditional databases (it's for large files, not transactional data)
Forgetting to account for data replication when calculating storage requirements
